text:
We know how to find the distance between two points if they
are on the
axes or if they f
 form a line segment parallel to the axes. For example, in
Fig. 1.5
5 we can find the distances
W,W, a
and SS2. What should we do if
the segment joining t
the points is not parallel to either axis? We can use
the Baudhayana-Pythagoras theorem which you have studied in Grade 8.
We can use this result to find the distance between any t
two points in the
xy-plane.
